original_abstract:
This article is dedicated to Professor George Lotter, whose mentorship has shaped the academic journeys of over 90 postgraduate students in Practical Theology at North-West University (NWU). Under his stewardship, a vast body of empirical work has emerged, reflecting a broader global and national shift toward empirical methodologies within the discipline. Recognizing the necessity for both educators and students to sharpen their proficiency in these methods, this article advocates for a more rigorous and systematic framework during the coding and data analysis phases of qualitative research. To this end, the author proposes a structured model for coding and data analysis specifically tailored for practical theological studies. 

Hierdie artikel word opgedra aan prof. George Lotter, wat ’n deurslaggewende rol gespeel het in die vorming van meer as 90 nagraadse studente in Praktiese Teologie by die Noord-Wes Universiteit (NWU). Onder sy leiding het ’n beduidende volume empiriese navorsing die lig gesien, wat aansluit by die nasionale en internasionale neiging tot ’n sterker empiriese fokus binne die vakgebied. Dit is daarom noodsaaklik dat dosente en studente hul vaardighede in hierdie navorsingsmetodologieë deurlopend opskerp. In hierdie bydrae word daar geargumenteer vir ’n meer sistematiese benadering tot kodering en data-analise binne kwalitatiewe navorsing, afgesluit met ’n voorgestelde model vir toepassing in prakties-teologiese studies.
humanized_abstract:
I dedicate this article to Professor George Lotter, who has supervised in excess of ninety postgraduate students in Practical Theology at the North-West University (NWU). He has played a seminal role in a diverse body of empirical work, a development that reflects the worldwide and local increase in empirical approaches in Practical Theology. An essential contribution to the empowerment of lecturers and students who must utilise these approaches on a regular basis is still required. This article urges that greater sophistication may be achieved if greater control and direction is achieved in particular stages of qualitative research, specifically during the processes of coding and data analysis. Consequently, the author develops a structured approach to coding and data analysis which could enrich the research enterprise in Practical Theology.

Die article is dedicated to prof. George Lotter who supervised more than 90 postgraduate students in Practical Theology at the North-West University (NWU). Under his leadership a substantial body of empirical research nationally and internationally adhered to the empirical orientation common to the discipline. It is imperative for lecturers and students to reflect on their competence in empirical research methodologies. In this article, an argument is made for a more systematic approach to coding and analysing data in qualitative research, and a suggested model is given for application in practical theology.
